title: Find Customer Referee
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal database challenge: the single SQL filter is also the speed extreme and memory extreme. Per the repo standard (no coincident files), only solution.sql is shipped; there is no separate solution-runtime or solution-memory file.

variants.languageReason: The task is a relational projection with a null-aware filter over one table. SQL evaluates the predicate inside the database engine, preserves native NULL semantics, and avoids client-side materialization; this is the fastest and leanest accepted interface for a LeetCode Database problem.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Not a LeetCode Database submission language for this schema; a client-side implementation would need to extract rows before applying a predicate the database can evaluate directly.
Unsupported for this database prompt, and manual row handling would add transfer and memory overhead compared with a pushed-down SQL filter.
Strong for native in-memory filtering, but unsupported here and dominated by executing the relational selection where the data resides.
Unsupported for this SQL schema submission; application-side slices or structs would materialize rows outside the database engine.
Not the accepted query interface for the database track; managed row objects and client-side filtering would be heavier than the SQL plan.

Python/Pandas can express the same filter with isna() plus inequality, but it materializes DataFrame rows; the other scripting languages are not the intended database submission path. SQL is leaner and optimizer-driven.

variants.approachJustification: Filter Customer rows with referee_id <> 2 or referee_id IS NULL, then project only name. The explicit NULL branch is required because SQL inequality does not match NULL values.
variants.timeComplexity: O(C) logical scan work without a referee_id index

reasoningSummary: The problem asks for every customer except those referred by id 2. A single null-aware WHERE predicate captures both allowed cases and avoids unnecessary joins, sorting, grouping, or computed expressions.
timeComplexity: O(C) logical scan work without a referee_id index
